dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8888 IH: Ending Passenger Rail Forced Arbitration Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Deluzio (for himself and Mr. Boyle of Pennsylvania) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To amend title 49, United States Code, to prohibit Amtrak from including mandatory arbitration clauses in contracts of carriage, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Ending Passenger Rail Forced Arbitration Act.

section.header: No validity or enforceability of arbitration agreements for consumer and civil rights disputes

subsection.text: Chapter 243 of title 49, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:
The purposes of this section are—

paragraph.text: to prohibit predispute arbitration agreements that force arbitration of consumer and civil rights disputes between Amtrak and customers of Amtrak; and

to prohibit agreements and practices that interfere with the right of customers to participate in a joint, class, or collective action related to consumer and civil rights disputes between Amtrak and customers of Amtrak.
The term Amtrak means the National Railroad Passenger Corporation.
The term civil rights dispute means a dispute—

subparagraph.text: arising from an alleged violation of—

clause.text: the Constitution of the United States or the constitution of a State; or
any Federal, State, or local law that prohibits discrimination on the basis of—

subclause.text: race, sex, age, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, religion, or national origin; or

any legally protected status in education, employment, credit, housing, public accommodations and facilities, voting, veterans and servicemembers, health care, or a program funded or conducted by the Federal Government or a State government, including any law referred to or described in section 62(e) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, including parts of such law not explicitly referenced in such section that relate to protecting individuals on any such basis; and

subparagraph.text: in which at least 1 party alleging a violation described in subparagraph (A) consists of 1 or more customers (or their authorized representative), including 1 or more individuals seeking certification as a class under rule 23 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure or a comparable rule or provision of State law.

paragraph.text: The term consumer dispute means any dispute, including all claims related to personal injuries, between Amtrak and 1 or more customers who seek or acquire—

subparagraph.text: services and accommodations provided by Amtrak; or
carriage on Amtrak trains and equipment.

paragraph.text: The term customer means any individual, except for an employee of Amtrak and without regard to whether the individual is a minor or paid for the transportation, who seeks or acquires—

subparagraph.text: services and accommodations provided by Amtrak; or
carriage on Amtrak trains and equipment.

paragraph.text: The term predispute arbitration agreement means an agreement to arbitrate a dispute that has not yet arisen at the time of the making of the agreement.

The term predispute joint-action waiver means an agreement, whether or not part of a predispute arbitration agreement, which would prohibit, or waive the right of, 1 of the parties to the agreement to participate in a joint, class, or collective action in a judicial, arbitral, administrative, or other forum, concerning a dispute that has not yet arisen at the time of the making of the agreement.
The term rail passenger carrier means a rail carrier providing—

subparagraph.text: intercity rail passenger transportation (as such term is defined in section 24102); or
interstate or intrastate high-speed rail (as such term is defined in section 26105) transportation, excluding a tourist, historic, scenic, or excursion rail carrier.

paragraph.text: All predispute arbitration agreements and predispute joint-action waivers shall be invalid and unenforceable with respect to a consumer or civil rights dispute between Amtrak (in its capacity as a rail passenger carrier) and a customer of Amtrak.

subparagraph.text: A determination of whether this section applies to a particular dispute shall be made in accordance with Federal law.

The applicability of this section to an agreement to arbitrate and the validity and enforceability of an agreement to which this section applies shall be determined by a court, rather than by an arbitrator, regardless of whether—

clause.text: the party resisting arbitration challenges the arbitration agreement specifically or in conjunction with other terms of the contract containing such agreement; and
the agreement purports to delegate such determinations to an arbitrator.

subparagraph.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to apply to a predispute arbitration agreement or joint-action waiver invoked in connection with any dispute subject to the Railway Labor Act (45 U.S.C. 151 et seq.).

paragraph.text: shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act; and
shall apply with respect to any dispute or claim that arises or accrues on or after such date.

subsection.text: The analysis for chapter 243 of title 49, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:
